terms.definition: Inflammation or degeneration of the plantar fascia at its calcaneal insertion, causing heel pain.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient described first-step morning heel pain typical of plantar fasciitis.
terms.contextNotes: First-step morning pain is the classic patient complaint.

terms.definition: Painful degenerative or overuse condition of the Achilles tendon, often mid-portion or insertional.
terms.exampleSentence: Mid-portion Achilles tendinopathy was managed with eccentric calf loading.
terms.contextNotes: 'Tendinopathy' preferred over 'tendinitis' as inflammation is variable.

terms.definition: Lateral deviation of the great toe at the first MTPJ with a prominent medial bony bump (bunion).
terms.exampleSentence: Hallux valgus deformity caused painful pressure against her shoe.
terms.contextNotes: 'Bunion' is the lay term used with patients.

terms.definition: Osteoarthritis of the first MTPJ causing painful, restricted dorsiflexion of the great toe.
terms.exampleSentence: Hallux rigidus limited her push-off and was managed with a stiff-soled shoe.
terms.contextNotes: Loss of dorsiflexion distinguishes it from hallux valgus.

terms.definition: Toe deformity with flexion at the proximal interphalangeal joint and extension at the MTPJ.
terms.exampleSentence: A painful corn formed over the dorsal PIPJ of the hammer toe.
terms.contextNotes: Flex at PIPJ; extend at MTPJ. Distal IPJ is straight.

terms.definition: Toe deformity with extension at the MTPJ and flexion at both proximal and distal interphalangeal joints.
terms.exampleSentence: Multiple claw toes contributed to her metatarsalgia.
terms.contextNotes: Often bilateral and seen in neuropathy or cavus feet.

terms.definition: Toe deformity with flexion at the distal interphalangeal joint only, often producing a tip callus.
terms.exampleSentence: A painful apical callus had formed beneath the mallet toe.
terms.contextNotes: Affects only the DIPJ — that distinguishes it from hammer toe.

terms.definition: Ingrowing toenail in which the nail plate penetrates the lateral nail fold causing inflammation.
terms.exampleSentence: Recurrent onychocryptosis of the hallux was treated with partial nail avulsion and phenolisation.
terms.contextNotes: Use 'ingrowing toenail' with patients; 'onychocryptosis' in writing.

terms.definition: Fungal infection of the nail plate or bed, usually by dermatophytes, causing thickening and discolouration.
terms.exampleSentence: Yellow, crumbly nails consistent with onychomycosis were confirmed on mycology.
terms.contextNotes: Confirm with mycology before starting prolonged systemic antifungals.

terms.definition: Dermatophyte infection of the foot causing itching, scaling and maceration, especially between the toes.
terms.exampleSentence: Interdigital maceration and itching pointed to tinea pedis.
terms.contextNotes: Use 'athlete's foot' with patients.

terms.definition: A plantar wart caused by HPV, presenting as a hyperkeratotic lesion with disrupted skin lines and punctate capillaries.
terms.exampleSentence: A painful plantar verruca with characteristic black dots was noted on the heel.
terms.contextNotes: Pinpoint capillaries on debridement help distinguish from corn.

terms.definition: Diffuse area of hyperkeratosis from chronic pressure or friction, typically on weight-bearing skin.
terms.exampleSentence: A diffuse callus was present beneath the second metatarsal head.
terms.contextNotes: Diffuse and broad; differs from a focal corn.

terms.definition: A focal cone-shaped area of hyperkeratosis with a central core, usually from localised pressure.
terms.exampleSentence: A hard corn over the dorsal PIPJ of the second toe required regular debridement.
terms.contextNotes: Hard corns sit over bony prominences; soft corns sit interdigitally.

terms.definition: Painful perineural fibrosis of an interdigital nerve, classically the third web space, causing forefoot pain.
terms.exampleSentence: A positive Mulder's click supported the diagnosis of Morton's neuroma.
terms.contextNotes: Classically affects the third web space; women > men.

terms.definition: Compression of the tibial nerve beneath the flexor retinaculum behind the medial malleolus.
terms.exampleSentence: Burning medial foot pain and a positive Tinel's sign indicated tarsal tunnel syndrome.
terms.contextNotes: Foot equivalent of carpal tunnel; nerve conduction studies aid diagnosis.

terms.definition: Inflammation of the sesamoid bones or their surrounding tissues beneath the first metatarsal head.
terms.exampleSentence: Sesamoiditis caused well-localised pain on weight-bearing under the great toe.
terms.contextNotes: Distinguish from sesamoid stress fracture on imaging.

terms.definition: Flat foot deformity with loss of the medial longitudinal arch, either flexible or rigid.
terms.exampleSentence: Bilateral flexible pes planus was managed with custom orthoses.
terms.contextNotes: Use 'flat feet' with patients.

terms.definition: High-arched foot with an exaggerated medial longitudinal arch, often associated with neurological disease.
terms.exampleSentence: Bilateral pes cavus and clawed toes prompted referral for neurological assessment.
terms.contextNotes: Bilateral cavus warrants screening for hereditary neuropathy.

terms.definition: Progressive neuropathic destruction of foot bones and joints, classically in long-standing diabetes.
terms.exampleSentence: A warm, swollen, deformed foot in a neuropathic patient raised suspicion of acute Charcot foot.
terms.contextNotes: Often misdiagnosed as cellulitis; offloading is the cornerstone of care.

terms.definition: A break in the skin of the foot in a person with diabetes, usually on a pressure area, predisposed by neuropathy and ischaemia.
terms.exampleSentence: A grade 2 diabetic foot ulcer beneath the first metatarsal head required total contact casting.
terms.contextNotes: Always assess perfusion, sensation and infection.

terms.definition: Crystal arthropathy caused by monosodium urate deposition, classically presenting with acute first MTPJ arthritis (podagra).
terms.exampleSentence: Acute, exquisitely tender swelling of the first MTPJ was diagnostic of gout.
terms.contextNotes: First MTPJ involvement is termed podagra.

terms.definition: Pain and tenderness localised to the metatarsal heads on the plantar forefoot.
terms.exampleSentence: Diffuse metatarsalgia improved with a metatarsal dome and footwear modification.
terms.contextNotes: A symptom, not a diagnosis — search for the underlying cause.

terms.definition: Acute or chronic infection of the nail fold, usually bacterial in acute and Candida-related in chronic forms.
terms.exampleSentence: A fluctuant paronychia of the hallux required incision and drainage.
terms.contextNotes: Often follows ingrowing toenail or minor trauma.

terms.definition: Painless detachment of the nail plate from the nail bed beginning distally.
terms.exampleSentence: Distal onycholysis with subungual debris suggested onychomycosis.
terms.contextNotes: Causes include trauma, fungal infection, psoriasis and thyroid disease.

terms.definition: Collection of blood beneath the nail plate, typically from a crush injury, causing throbbing pain.
terms.exampleSentence: Painful subungual haematoma involving more than 50% of the nail bed was trephined.
terms.contextNotes: Note British spelling 'haematoma'.

terms.definition: Disruption of the fibrocartilaginous plate beneath a lesser MTPJ, causing pain, swelling and toe deviation.
terms.exampleSentence: A second-toe plantar plate tear was confirmed on ultrasound.
terms.contextNotes: Suspect with a positive drawer test at the lesser MTPJ.

terms.definition: Stretching or tearing of ankle ligaments, most commonly the anterior talofibular ligament after inversion injury.
terms.exampleSentence: A grade 2 lateral ankle sprain was managed with functional rehabilitation.
terms.contextNotes: Use Ottawa rules to decide on radiographs.

terms.definition: Damage to peripheral nerves causing sensory loss, paraesthesia or weakness, often in a stocking distribution in the feet.
terms.exampleSentence: Reduced 10-g monofilament sensation indicated significant peripheral neuropathy.
terms.contextNotes: Underlies most diabetic foot complications.

terms.definition: Atherosclerotic narrowing of lower-limb arteries causing claudication, rest pain or critical limb ischaemia.
terms.exampleSentence: Absent pedal pulses and an ABPI of 0.6 confirmed peripheral arterial disease.
terms.contextNotes: Critical for assessing diabetic foot ulceration risk.

terms.definition: Acute bacterial infection of skin and subcutaneous tissue presenting with erythema, warmth, swelling and pain.
terms.exampleSentence: Spreading cellulitis of the foot required intravenous flucloxacillin.
terms.contextNotes: Outline the erythema with a marker pen to monitor progression.
